title: pleopeltis selliguea
intro: Pleopeltis selliguea, also known as the Selliguea polypody or the Selliguea fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Pleopeltis selliguea include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Pleopeltis selliguea include decoctions, infusions, and topical applications, which can be made by soaking the plant's leaves or rhizomes in water or other solvents to release its active compounds.
